reasoning: The resolution requires two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth with the start occurring between Q1 2024 and Q4 2025. Based on historical data from the BEA up to late February 2026, real GDP grew in all quarters of 2024 and 2025: Q1 2024 (+1.6% advance), Q2 2024 (+3.0%), Q3 2024 (+2.8%), Q4 2024 (+2.4%), Q2 2025 (+3.3%), Q3 2025 (+4.3%), and Q4 2025 (+1.4% advance). Because consecutive positive growth completely dominates the 2024-2025 period, there is near-zero chance (within possible revisions) of there being two consecutive negative quarters. Thus, the US has verifiably not entered a recession based on the resolution criteria.
